<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc"></a>

# VisualShaderNodeDerivativeFunc

**Inherits:** [VisualShaderNode](class_visualshadernode.md#class-visualshadernode) **<** [Resource](class_resource.md#class-resource) **<** [RefCounted](class_refcounted.md#class-refcounted) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

Calculates a derivative within the visual shader graph.

## Description

This node is only available in `Fragment` and `Light` visual shaders.

## Properties

| [Function](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-function)   | [function](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-property-function)   | `0`   |
|-------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
| [OpType](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-optype)       | [op_type](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-property-op-type)     | `0`   |
| [Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) | [precision](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-property-precision) | `0`   |

---

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-func-x"></a>

[Function](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-function) **FUNC_X** = `1`

Derivative in `x` using local differencing.

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-func-y"></a>

[Function](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-function) **FUNC_Y** = `2`

Derivative in `y` using local differencing.

<a id="enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision"></a>

enum **Precision**: [🔗](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision)

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-none"></a>

[Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) **PRECISION_NONE** = `0`

No precision is specified, the GPU driver is allowed to use whatever level of precision it chooses. This is the default option and is equivalent to using `dFdx()` or `dFdy()` in text shaders.

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-coarse"></a>

[Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) **PRECISION_COARSE** = `1`

The derivative will be calculated using the current fragment's neighbors (which may not include the current fragment). This tends to be faster than using [PRECISION_FINE](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-fine), but may not be suitable when more precision is needed. This is equivalent to using `dFdxCoarse()` or `dFdyCoarse()` in text shaders.

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-fine"></a>

[Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) **PRECISION_FINE** = `2`

The derivative will be calculated using the current fragment and its immediate neighbors. This tends to be slower than using [PRECISION_COARSE](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-coarse), but may be necessary when more precision is needed. This is equivalent to using `dFdxFine()` or `dFdyFine()` in text shaders.

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-constant-precision-max"></a>

[Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) **PRECISION_MAX** = `3`

Represents the size of the [Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) enum.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-property-precision"></a>

[Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision) **precision** = `0` [🔗](#class-visualshadernodederivativefunc-property-precision)

-  **set_precision**(value: [Precision](#enum-visualshadernodederivativefunc-precision))
